deep_dive.title: The Hidden Footprint: Making Storage a First-Class Metric for LLM Agent Evaluation
deep_dive.abstract: LLM agent benchmarks measure task completion, reliability, and inference cost, but not the persistent data an agent run leaves on disk, including logs, context snapshots, checkpoints, and debug traces. We introduce AgentFootprint, a cross-framework benchmark of post-run agent storage footprint. Its serialization-aware metric suite measures total retention, channel composition, duplication, growth, compressibility, and conversation-history reconstructability. It addresses a measurement trap: naive byte-level measurement understates duplication by an order of magnitude because database paging and JSON escaping obscure repeated content. A fixed-trace control separates agent-generated logical volume from persistence-layer amplification: replaying the same trajectory through seven persisting frameworks yields a 6.7x spread. Under identical models, tools, and tasks, configurations with 100% accuracy differ by 15.7x in retained bytes, although their defaults support different recovery and audit capabilities. Three full-history configurations grow superlinearly on a repeated-observation stress task. Exported trajectories from 108 instance-normalized SWE-bench Verified submissions span three orders of magnitude per instance, with no detectable correlation with resolve rate. A content-addressed store reduces retention by 4.8x-32.7x while preserving every reconstructability score. These results establish persistent storage as a resource metric to report jointly with accuracy and reconstructability.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: storage footprint metric for LLM agent evaluation relates to memory persistence
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 存储足迹指标与LLM智能体记忆持久化相关
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 存储足迹指标与LLM智能体记忆持久化相关
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 存储足迹指标与LLM智能体记忆持久化相关
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: AgentFootprint measures post-run storage footprint of LLM agents, addressing a gap in evaluation metrics by including logs, snapshots and checkpoints.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有LLM智能体评估忽略运行后产生的持久数据，如日志、快照和检查点。本文提出AgentFootprint，一个跨框架的存储足迹基准，通过序列化感知的指标套件测量重复、压缩比和会话历史可重构性。实验显示朴素字节级测量低估重复量一个数量级，固定轨迹控制可分离逻辑体积与持久层放大，使存储成为智能体评估的一级指标。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 现有LLM智能体评估忽略运行后产生的持久数据，如日志、快照和检查点。本文提出AgentFootprint，一个跨框架的存储足迹基准，通过序列化感知的指标套件测量重复、压缩比和会话历史可重构性。实验显示朴素字节级测量低估重复量一个数量级，固定轨迹控制可分离逻辑体积与持久层放大，使存储成为智能体评估的一级指标。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: How is memory evaluated in LLM agent systems?

quick_skim.title: VTM-Nav: Hierarchical Visual-Topological Memory for Cross-Episode Object-Goal Navigation
quick_skim.abstract: Object-goal navigation requires an embodied agent to locate and reach an instance of a specified object category in an indoor environment. Recent training-free approaches leverage vision-language models (VLMs) for open-vocabulary semantic reasoning, but are typically evaluated under an episodic protocol that resets all scene-specific state after each episode. We introduce Cross-Episode Object-Goal Navigation, in which an agent repeatedly operates in the same scene, retains only self-acquired experience, and keeps its model parameters fixed. To support experience reuse, we present \method, a training-free VLM navigation framework with a persistent hierarchical Visual-Topological Memory (VTM). The VTM organizes scene knowledge at room and object levels and retrieves relevant experience through coarse-to-fine matching, providing memory as soft guidance only when it agrees with current observations. A conservative execution guard further mitigates oscillations, blocked motions, and premature stopping. Under a controlled same-scene protocol, we evaluate \method{} on three benchmarks, HM3D v0.1, HM3D v0.2, and MP3D, and compare it with a strengthened WMNav baseline augmented with cross-episode textual memory, while keeping the VLM backbone and action pipeline identical. \method{} achieves the best performance across all three benchmarks, demonstrating the effectiveness and robustness of structured visual-topological experience reuse across datasets.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: persistent hierarchical Visual-Topological Memory for cross-episode navigation
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 用于跨情景导航的持久分层视觉-拓扑记忆
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 用于跨情景导航的持久分层视觉-拓扑记忆
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 用于跨情景导航的持久分层视觉-拓扑记忆
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes VTM-Nav with a persistent hierarchical Visual-Topological Memory enabling VLMs to reuse experience across episodes in object-goal navigation.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文针对物体目标导航中情景性重置导致经验无法复用的问题，提出跨情景物体目标导航任务。方法VTM-Nav采用持久分层视觉-拓扑记忆（VTM），在房间和物体级别组织场景知识，允许智能体在多次情景中积累和利用经验。实验表明，该记忆机制显著提高了导航效率，是记忆增强在具身智能体中的成功应用。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文针对物体目标导航中情景性重置导致经验无法复用的问题，提出跨情景物体目标导航任务。方法VTM-Nav采用持久分层视觉-拓扑记忆（VTM），在房间和物体级别组织场景知识，允许智能体在多次情景中积累和利用经验。实验表明，该记忆机制显著提高了导航效率，是记忆增强在具身智能体中的成功应用。

quick_skim.title: Step-Level Preference Learning for Generative Agents in Social Simulations
quick_skim.abstract: Large language model (LLM)-based generative agents simulate human behavior through long-horizon decision-making processes that comprise intermediate steps such as planning, memory retrieval, reflection, and action selection. However, fine-grained human annotations of these intermediate steps remain scarce, and existing agents are not grounded in human preferences over such intermediate decisions. To address this gap, we introduce \method, an interactive simulation interface that enables us to collect step-level human preference supervision over agent decision trajectories, leading to a dataset of 57K fine-grained annotations. We conduct step-level preference learning on open-weight language models using supervised finetuning and direct preference optimization on this data, consistently improving simulation fidelity, coordination, and interaction quality, and inducing more socially effective agent behavior. Our results show that step-level human supervision is an effective training signal for improving both local decision quality and long-horizon agent behavior.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: addresses memory retrieval and reflection in generative agent decision steps
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 涉及生成式agent决策步骤中的记忆检索与反思
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 涉及生成式agent决策步骤中的记忆检索与反思
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 涉及生成式agent决策步骤中的记忆检索与反思
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Step-Level Preference Learning collects human preferences on intermediate steps including memory retrieval and reflection, and fine-tunes LLMs to align with them.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对生成式智能体社会模拟中的中间步骤（如记忆检索、反思）收集人类偏好，构建了57K细粒度标注数据集。通过监督微调和直接偏好优化，使LLM的决策步骤与人类偏好对齐。实验表明，步骤级偏好学习显著提升了智能体行为的自然度和合理性。该工作为agent记忆相关行为的优化提供了数据驱动方法。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文针对生成式智能体社会模拟中的中间步骤（如记忆检索、反思）收集人类偏好，构建了57K细粒度标注数据集。通过监督微调和直接偏好优化，使LLM的决策步骤与人类偏好对齐。实验表明，步骤级偏好学习显著提升了智能体行为的自然度和合理性。该工作为agent记忆相关行为的优化提供了数据驱动方法。
